name: iris-jwst

metadata.name: iris-jwst
metadata.summary: iris-jwst is a GPU-accelerated package to generate high-resolution opacity-weighted molecular line intensities to model disk spectra.

documentation:
# iris-jwst

iris-jwst is a GPU-accelerated package to generate high-resolution opacity-weighted molecular line intensities to model JWST MIRI MRS spectra.

classes.name: iris.slab

methods.name: iris.slab.setup_disk
documentation:
setup_disk: setup the disk physical structure

:distance: distance to source (float)
:T_ex: excitation temperature (array)
:N_mol: column density in cm^-2 (array)
:A_au: emitting area in au^2 (array)
:dV: intrinsic line FWHM in km/s (array)

:inc: disk inclination in degree (float), face-on = 0, experimental
:M_star: stellar mass in Msun (float), experimental
:r_in: inner gas radius in au (array), experimental. An ARRAY of the inner emission 
       radius for EACH molecule included in the model

The structure of each physical parameter must be an array of 
shape (M, N), where N is the number of slabs and M the number of species. 

For now iris does not support using a different number of temperature components 
for each species modeled simultaneously.

methods.name: iris.slab.setup_grid
documentation:
setup_grid: setup the wavelength grid

:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate the opacity
:obs_wgrid: wavelength grid used to downsample the model
:R: the instrumental resolving power

----------------------------------------------------------------
To correctly sample overlapping lines, make sure that the 
wavelength spacing in fine_wgrid appropiately samples the intrinsic
line width. Ideally you want a few points per 
intrinsic width, dlambda<1e-5 micron usually works well for MIRI.

To avoid a nan catastrophe, make sure that obs_wgrid does not span
outside the range of fine_wgrid.

methods.name: iris.slab.convolve
documentation:
convolution (internal function called by iris.simulate)
user shouldn't call this directly

methods.name: iris.slab.downsample
documentation:
downsampling (internal function called by iris.simulate)
user shouldn't call this directly

methods.name: iris.slab.simulate
documentation: Simulate a convolved and downsampled spectrum

methods.name: iris.slab.simulate_keplerian
documentation:
Simulate a convolved and downsampled spectrum including Keplerian line profiles
Requires inc, M_star, and r_in to be set up first.
Experimental

slab: generate an object to model spectra

:molecules: list of molecules to include in the model (list of strings)
:wlow: shortest wavelength in micron (float) 
:whigh:  longest wavelength in micron (float) 
:path_to_moldata: path to folder with HITRAN molecular data (string)

functions.name: iris.setup.setup_linelists
documentation:
setup_linelists: Downloads data from HITRAN using astroquery

:molecule_name: name of the specific molecule (e.g. 13CO2) (string)
:base_name: name of the main isotopolog (e.g. CO2) (string)
:isotopologue: isotopolog number in HITRAN (e.g. 2 for 13CO2) (float)
:path_to_moldata: path to the folder to download the molecular data file (string)
Code adapted from Nathan Hagen
https://github.com/nzhagen/hitran

modules.name: iris.moldata
documentation:
The class MolData reads and parses the molecular data

* Molecular data in the same format as for the Fortran 90 code are used. They consist of a block for the
  partition function and a block for the lines
* Both partition function and lines are stored as named tuples
* Only read access to the fields is granted through properties

- 01/06/2020: SB, initial version
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
HITRAN MOLECULAR DATA PARSER
--- THIS SECTION ADAPTED FROM CODE WRITTEN BY SIMON BRUDERER (2020) ----

functions.name: iris.moldata.setup_catalog
documentation: Set up with iris (CEM-R)

methods.name: iris.moldata.MolData.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a molecular data structure and read from a .par file. The file has the same structure as in the
Fortran 90 code, see notes below.

Parameters
----------
name: str
    Label of the molecule (e.g. "CO")
fname: str
    Path/filename of the .par file

Notes
-----

The file format of .par file is as follows:

# Some comments, followed by the number of partition function values
2933
# Some further comments, followed by the partition function (temperature vs Q(temperature))
70.0    25.544319
71.0    25.904587
...
# Some further comments, followed by the number of lines
754
# Some further comments followed by the lines
1      1_R_13         1_R_13  196.29472  1527.25685208   1.3500e-04     3565.78714     3492.49079   58.0   54.0
2      0_R_13         0_R_13  194.54560  1540.98815610   2.3660e-04      554.97254      481.01720   58.0   54.0

Important: The lines section on the bottom is *fixed format*:
- Nr: Index of the line - integer, 6 characters
- Lev_up: Upper level label - string, 15 characters
- Lev_low: Lower level label - string, 15 characters
- Lam: Wavelength in micron - float, 11 characters
- Freq: Frequency in GHz - float, 15 characters
- A_stein: Einstein-A in s**-1 - float, 13 characters
- E_up: Upper level energy in K - float, 15 characters
- E_low: Lower level energy in K - float, 15 characters
- g_up: Upper level statistical weight - float, 7 characters
- g_low: Lower level statistical weight - float, 7 characters

methods.name: iris.moldata.MolData._read_molecule
documentation:
Reads the molecular data file, should be only called at initialization

Parameters
----------
fname: str
    Path/filename of the .par file

Returns
-------
_partition_type:
    Partition function
_lines_type:
    Lines

modules.name: iris.spectrum
documentation:
--------------------------------------------------------------
IRIS: (GPU-accelerated) IR spectrum modeling

Evaluation of optical depths and intensities is jit-enabled and
vectorized. 
--------------------------------------------------------------
Developed by Carlos E. Romero-Mirza (2024)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.J_profile
documentation:
J_profile: full (blackbody) Plack function

```
wavelength

wavelength in micron

t_ex

temperature in K

return

blackbody flux density in erg cm-2 s-1 Hz-1 sr-1
```

functions.name: iris.spectrum.dv_to_dlam
documentation:
dv_to_dlam: convert velocity element in km/s to wavelength element in micron,
            given a reference wavelength
:dv: elocity element in km/s
:lam_rest: reference wavelength in micron

returns: dlam in micron

functions.name: iris.spectrum.keplerian_velocity
documentation: Calculate the Keplerian velocity at radius r for a star of mass M_star.

functions.name: iris.spectrum.line_of_sight_velocity
documentation:
Calculate the line-of-sight velocity for a given radius r, azimuthal angle phi,
inclination i, and stellar mass M_star.

functions.name: iris.spectrum.keplerian_broadening_kernel
documentation:
Construct the Keplerian broadening kernel for a disk with inner radius r_in,
outer radius r_out, stellar mass M_star, and inclination i.

functions.name: iris.spectrum.evaluate_line_tau
documentation:
evaluate_line_tau: get line optical depth profile 

:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 
:sigma_lam: intrinsic line (gaussian) width in micron
:tau_cen: optical depth at line center
:line_w: wavelenbgth at line center in micron

returns: tau (unitless)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_tau_grid
documentation:
compute_tau_grid: get optical depth profile for all lines in catalog

:line_catalog: HITRAN catalog 
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 
:dv: intrinsic line FWHM in km/s
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K
:n_mol: column density in cm^-2

returns: tau (unitless)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_total_tau
documentation:
compute_tau_grid: get optical depth profile for all molecules

:catalog: HITRAN catalogs
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 
:dv: intrinsic line FWHM in km/s
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K
:n_mol: column density in cm^-2

returns: tau (unitless)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_fdens
documentation:
compute_fdens: get flux density for one species

:distance: distance to source in pc
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 
:total_tau: tau as function of wavelength
:A_au: emitting area in au^2
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K

returns: flux density (erg cm-2 s-1 Hz-1)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_fdens_keplerian
documentation:
compute_fdens: get flux density for one species, including keplerian profile

:distance: distance to source in pc
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 
:total_tau: tau as function of wavelength
:A_au: emitting area in au^2
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K
:r_in: innermost radii in au
:M_star: stellar mass in solar mass
:inc: inclination in radian

returns: flux density (erg cm-2 s-1 Hz-1)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_total_fdens
documentation:
compute_total_fdens: get total flux density in Jy

:catalog: HITRAN catalog (dict)
:distance: distance to source in pc
:A_au: emitting area in au^2
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K
:n_mol: column density in cm^-2
:dv: intrinsic line FWHM in km/s
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 

returns: flux density (Jy)

functions.name: iris.spectrum.compute_total_fdens_keplerian
documentation:
compute_total_fdens_keplerian: get total flux density in Jy including Keplerian profile

:catalog: HITRAN catalog (dict)
:distance: distance to source in pc
:A_au: emitting area in au^2
:t_ex: excitation temperature in K
:n_mol: column density in cm^-2
:dv: intrinsic line FWHM in km/s
:r_in: innermost radii in au
:M_star: stellar mass in solar mass
:inc: inclination in radian
:fine_wgrid: fine wavelength grid to evaluate tau 

returns: flux density (Jy)
